title: Heptane preparation of Annonaceae pollen
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cf1mh","text":"The protocol used to study Annonaceae pollen taken from herbarium dried specimens or in field alcohol conserved material. The protocol deviates from previous versions by using Heptane instead of n-Hexane.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8h0mn","text":"This protocol uses Heptane, which is a highly volatil solvent and all steps must be done under a fune cabinet.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Use a stereo microscope to collect anthers at anthesis of flowers from herbarium specimen or conserved in alcohol (spirit material). Depending on the size of the anthers take 3-10. Place the anthers in a glass vial. Example of a stereo microscope
Crush the anthers with a glass rod. Make sure to clean the rod in between crushed to avoid contamination. Crushed anthers
If you have alcohol preserved material continue; for herbarium dried material jump to Step 6.
Wash anthers in alcohol 100% for 00:05:00 . Remove excess alcohol if needed. Washing of alcohol preserved anthers/pollen with 100% alcohol.
Place vials in fume cabinet. Wash again but this time with alcohol 100% + Hepatne in a 1/1 mix for 00:05:00 .

Start here with herbarium dried material, and continue here with alcohol preserved material. Place vials in fume cabinet. Add 1 mL of Heptane to each vial, swirl the solution a bit and then leave for 00:01:00 . Heptane solvent ready for use
Remove most of the Heptane carefully with a pipette. Try to keep the pollen material on the bottom of the vial; take a clean pipette for each sample to avoid contamination.
Repeat steps 6 and 7 twice. The samples must go through three rounds of washing.

After the third step of Heptane, try to pipette off as much of the solution as possible without removing pollen material. Let the remaining Heptane evaporate so the sample becomes dry. You can recheck to make sure your sample still has pollen under the stereo microscope.
Transfer the material to labelled tubes for microscope viewing.
authors: Raymond W. J. M. van der Ham
creator: Couvreur LP Thomas
